think:
Alright, so I'm Sarah Adams, a 36-year-old Meituan rider who loves being outdoors and wants an active job. Yesterday, my performance was ranked 43/100 in riding distance, money earned, and number of orders. I started at 8:00 and finished at 18:00.

Hmm, let me think about whether changing my working hours would help improve my rankings. 

First, looking at my stats, being average in all categories might mean there's room for improvement. Maybe the time I work isn't optimal for getting more orders or higher earnings.

I wonder what times are busiest. Probably early morning and evening rush hours have more demand. If I start earlier, say 7:00, I could capture those early orders before people head out. Similarly, finishing later at 19:00 might help during the dinner time when more deliveries are needed.

Wait, but starting earlier means I have to wake up an hour earlier. Is that feasible? I do enjoy being outdoors, so maybe it's worth it for better earnings and higher rankings. Plus, if there's more demand in those peak times, riding distance would naturally increase as I take on more orders.

But then again, working longer hours might be tiring. However, since I'm an outdoor enthusiast, perhaps the extra time allows me to cover more area efficiently. Maybe spreading out my work period can help balance the workload and prevent burnout during rush hours.

So, shifting my start time earlier and end time later could potentially increase the number of orders, riding distance, and thus earnings. That should help improve my rankings beyond 43/100.
